news.headline: Microsoft joins AI-driven tech layoff wave with 4,800 job cuts
news.summary: Microsoft is cutting about 2.1% of its workforce, or roughly 4,800 jobs, the latest in a wave of tech layoffs as the Windows maker spends heavily ‌on AI infrastructure and uses the technology to improve efficiency across its business.  Big Tech's ‌historic AI outlays, set to top $700 billion this year, are piling pressure on companies to show returns from the technology and ​offset the rising cost of rolling it out across their businesses.  Microsoft announced the cuts on Monday following a rough stretch, with its shares falling nearly 23% in the first six months of 2026, their worst first-half performance since 2022.

news.headline: AI investors may pivot to hyperscalers from chipmakers, Morgan Stanley says
news.summary: Morgan Stanley said the recent weakness in U.S. semiconductor stocks is a sign that the market ‌gains are broadening, with investors likely to turn toward ‌AI "hyperscalers" as well as consumer discretionary, transport and biotechnology shares.  In a note dated ​Monday, the brokerage said hyperscalers — an industry term for tech companies that are spending big on data centers — could benefit from a rotation away from semiconductor stocks as the AI cycle shifts.  Although the ‌likes of Alphabet ⁠and Amazon have committed billions to scale up their AI infrastructure, skyrocketing the share prices of semiconductor ⁠companies, clear evidence that AI products can generate returns that justify the spending is yet to be seen.

news.headline: IBM (IBM) Reaches First Quantum Milestone In Fusion Material Design
news.summary: IBM (NYSE:IBM), Oak Ridge National Laboratory and Cleveland Clinic completed the first quantum computer driven computations for fusion energy material design. The work positions IBM at the center of the U.S. Department of Energy’s Genesis Mission for quantum centric supercomputing. The collaboration targets molecular configurations critical for fusion reactors, an area that classical computing has not addressed. For investors following IBM, this move places the company’s quantum computing...

news.headline: Oak Ridge National Lab, Cleveland Clinic, and IBM Achieve First-Known Computations of Fusion Materials on a Quantum Computer
news.summary: A team of scientists from Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL), Cleveland Clinic, and IBM (NYSE: IBM), have calculated nine molecular configurations of a promising material to produce fuel for fusion energy – the first-known instance of such computations on quantum computers.

news.headline: Wall Street Got the Weak Jobs Report It Wanted. Chip Stocks Wrecked the Rally Anyway.
news.summary: Thursday's jobs report came in far below forecasts, initially easing pressure on the Federal Reserve, before a fresh slide in memory and semiconductor stocks dragged the Nasdaq lower even as the Dow closed at a record. Tesla sold off despite its best delivery quarter in years, healthcare names caught a bid, and SK Hynix lined up what would be the largest US listing ever by a foreign company.
